# Extracao: transcription_mp4_17__They_Don_t_Like_You

- **Fonte:** transcription_mp4_17__They_Don_t_Like_You.txt
- **Tipo:** TXT
- **Tamanho:** 0 MB

---

Look, these people don't like you. They don't like you. I can't tell you. I mean, I hate to even think about how many situations I've been in where they're just not buying from me because somebody doesn't like me. They don't like the way I stand, the way I talk, what I said. They don't like when I showed up or left. They don't like the email I send or the way I post something. They don't like me, okay? They don't like me and until either they go away or somebody changes their mind or I figure out how to get everybody on the planet to like me, they're probably not buying from me. Look, this isn't a negative. It is what it is. There's the transactional realities of the real world. Not everybody's going to like you. And if they did, look, if everybody liked you, you'd end up not liking yourself, okay? So I believe this. It's better for me to like myself, the activities I do every day, the discipline I have, what I show up and do, how I go out into the universe. It's more important for me to like me than everybody else to like me. And if it means I lose a deal, well, so be it, I lose a deal. But you could call them out on it. You could literally ask somebody, is there somebody in the company that just doesn't like me?
